showFullCode.title: Show Complete File
showFullCode.description: Show complete file for code, with bootstrap and error handling included.

BatchGetNamedQueryInput.title: BatchGetNamedQueryInput
BatchGetNamedQueryInput.description: Contains an array of named query IDs.

BatchGetQueryExecutionInput.title: BatchGetQueryExecutionInput
BatchGetQueryExecutionInput.description: Contains an array of query execution IDs.

AclConfiguration.title: AclConfiguration
AclConfiguration.description: Indicates that an Amazon S3 canned ACL should be set to control ownership of stored query results. When Athena stores query results in Amazon S3, the canned ACL is set with the x-amz-acl request header. For more information about S3 Object Ownership, see Object Ownership settings in the Amazon S3 User Guide.

ApplicationDPUSizes.title: ApplicationDPUSizes
ApplicationDPUSizes.description: Contains the application runtime IDs and their supported DPU sizes.

AthenaError.title: AthenaError
AthenaError.description: Provides information about an Athena query error. The AthenaError feature provides standardized error information to help you understand failed queries and take steps after a query failure occurs. AthenaError includes an ErrorCategory field that specifies whether the cause of the failed query is due to system error, user error, or other error.

NamedQuery.title: NamedQuery
NamedQuery.description: A query, where QueryString contains the SQL statements that make up the query.

UnprocessedNamedQueryId.title: UnprocessedNamedQueryId
UnprocessedNamedQueryId.description: Information about a named query ID that could not be processed.

PreparedStatement.title: PreparedStatement
PreparedStatement.description: A prepared SQL statement for use with Athena.

UnprocessedPreparedStatementName.title: UnprocessedPreparedStatementName
UnprocessedPreparedStatementName.description: The name of a prepared statement that could not be returned.

QueryExecution.title: QueryExecution
QueryExecution.description: Information about a single instance of a query execution.

UnprocessedQueryExecutionId.title: UnprocessedQueryExecutionId
UnprocessedQueryExecutionId.description: Describes a query execution that failed to process.

CalculationConfiguration.title: CalculationConfiguration
CalculationConfiguration.description: Contains configuration information for the calculation.

CalculationResult.title: CalculationResult
CalculationResult.description: Contains information about an application-specific calculation result.

CalculationStatistics.title: CalculationStatistics
CalculationStatistics.description: Contains statistics for a notebook calculation.

CalculationStatus.title: CalculationStatus
CalculationStatus.description: Contains information about the status of a notebook calculation.

CalculationSummary.title: CalculationSummary
CalculationSummary.description: Summary information for a notebook calculation.

Column.title: Column
Column.description: Contains metadata for a column in a table.

ColumnInfo.title: ColumnInfo
ColumnInfo.description: Information about the columns in a query execution result.

Tag.title: Tag
Tag.description: A label that you assign to a resource. In Athena, a resource can be a workgroup or data catalog. Each tag consists of a key and an optional value, both of which you define. For example, you can use tags to categorize Athena workgroups or data catalogs by purpose, owner, or environment. Use a consistent set of tag keys to make it easier to search and filter workgroups or data catalogs in your account. For best practices, see Tagging Best Practices. Tag keys can be from 1 to 128 UTF-8 Unicode characters, and tag values can be from 0 to 256 UTF-8 Unicode characters. Tags can use letters and numbers representable in UTF-8, and the following characters: + - = . _ : / @. Tag keys and values are case-sensitive. Tag keys must be unique per resource. If you specify more than one tag, separate them by commas.

WorkGroupConfiguration.title: WorkGroupConfiguration
WorkGroupConfiguration.description: The configuration of the workgroup, which includes the location in Amazon S3 where query and calculation results are stored, the encryption option, if any, used for query and calculation results, whether the Amazon CloudWatch Metrics are enabled for the workgroup and whether workgroup settings override query settings, and the data usage limits for the amount of data scanned per query or per workgroup. The workgroup settings override is specified in EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration (true/false) in the WorkGroupConfiguration. See WorkGroupConfiguration$EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration.

CustomerContentEncryptionConfiguration.title: CustomerContentEncryptionConfiguration
CustomerContentEncryptionConfiguration.description: Specifies the KMS key that is used to encrypt the user's data stores in Athena.

DataCatalog.title: DataCatalog
DataCatalog.description:
Contains information about a data catalog in an Amazon Web Services account.

In the Athena console, data catalogs are listed as "data sources" on the Data sources page under the Data source name column.

DataCatalogSummary.title: DataCatalogSummary
DataCatalogSummary.description: The summary information for the data catalog, which includes its name and type.

Database.title: Database
Database.description: Contains metadata information for a database in a data catalog.

Datum.title: Datum
Datum.description: A piece of data (a field in the table).

EncryptionConfiguration.title: EncryptionConfiguration
EncryptionConfiguration.description: If query and calculation results are encrypted in Amazon S3, indicates the encryption option used (for example, SSE_KMS or CSE_KMS) and key information.

EngineConfiguration.title: EngineConfiguration
EngineConfiguration.description: Contains data processing unit (DPU) configuration settings and parameter mappings for a notebook engine.

EngineVersion.title: EngineVersion
EngineVersion.description: The Athena engine version for running queries, or the PySpark engine version for running sessions.

ExecutorsSummary.title: ExecutorsSummary
ExecutorsSummary.description: Contains summary information about an executor.

NotebookMetadata.title: NotebookMetadata
NotebookMetadata.description: Contains metadata for notebook, including the notebook name, ID, workgroup, and time created.

FilterDefinition.title: FilterDefinition
FilterDefinition.description: A string for searching notebook names.

ResultSet.title: ResultSet
ResultSet.description: The metadata and rows that make up a query result set. The metadata describes the column structure and data types. To return a ResultSet object, use GetQueryResults.

QueryRuntimeStatistics.title: QueryRuntimeStatistics
QueryRuntimeStatistics.description: The query execution timeline, statistics on input and output rows and bytes, and the different query stages that form the query execution plan.

SessionStatus.title: SessionStatus
SessionStatus.description: Contains information about the status of a session.

WorkGroup.title: WorkGroup
WorkGroup.description: A workgroup, which contains a name, description, creation time, state, and other configuration, listed under WorkGroup$Configuration. Each workgroup enables you to isolate queries for you or your group of users from other queries in the same account, to configure the query results location and the encryption configuration (known as workgroup settings), to enable sending query metrics to Amazon CloudWatch, and to establish per-query data usage control limits for all queries in a workgroup. The workgroup settings override is specified in EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration (true/false) in the WorkGroupConfiguration. See WorkGroupConfiguration$EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration.

NotebookSessionSummary.title: NotebookSessionSummary
NotebookSessionSummary.description: Contains the notebook session ID and notebook session creation time.

PreparedStatementSummary.title: PreparedStatementSummary
PreparedStatementSummary.description: The name and last modified time of the prepared statement.

SessionSummary.title: SessionSummary
SessionSummary.description: Contains summary information about a session.

WorkGroupSummary.title: WorkGroupSummary
WorkGroupSummary.description: The summary information for the workgroup, which includes its name, state, description, and the date and time it was created.

ResultConfiguration.title: ResultConfiguration
ResultConfiguration.description: The location in Amazon S3 where query and calculation results are stored and the encryption option, if any, used for query and calculation results. These are known as "client-side settings". If workgroup settings override client-side settings, then the query uses the workgroup settings.

ResultReuseConfiguration.title: ResultReuseConfiguration
ResultReuseConfiguration.description: Specifies the query result reuse behavior for the query.

QueryExecutionContext.title: QueryExecutionContext
QueryExecutionContext.description: The database and data catalog context in which the query execution occurs.

QueryExecutionStatus.title: QueryExecutionStatus
QueryExecutionStatus.description: The completion date, current state, submission time, and state change reason (if applicable) for the query execution.

QueryExecutionStatistics.title: QueryExecutionStatistics
QueryExecutionStatistics.description: The amount of data scanned during the query execution and the amount of time that it took to execute, and the type of statement that was run.

ResultReuseInformation.title: ResultReuseInformation
ResultReuseInformation.description: Contains information about whether the result of a previous query was reused.

QueryRuntimeStatisticsTimeline.title: QueryRuntimeStatisticsTimeline
QueryRuntimeStatisticsTimeline.description: Timeline statistics such as query queue time, planning time, execution time, service processing time, and total execution time.

QueryRuntimeStatisticsRows.title: QueryRuntimeStatisticsRows
QueryRuntimeStatisticsRows.description: Statistics such as input rows and bytes read by the query, rows and bytes output by the query, and the number of rows written by the query.

QueryStage.title: QueryStage
QueryStage.description: Stage statistics such as input and output rows and bytes, execution time and stage state. This information also includes substages and the query stage plan.

QueryStagePlanNode.title: QueryStagePlanNode
QueryStagePlanNode.description: Stage plan information such as name, identifier, sub plans, and remote sources.

ResultConfigurationUpdates.title: ResultConfigurationUpdates
ResultConfigurationUpdates.description: The information about the updates in the query results, such as output location and encryption configuration for the query results.

ResultReuseByAgeConfiguration.title: ResultReuseByAgeConfiguration
ResultReuseByAgeConfiguration.description: Specifies whether previous query results are reused, and if so, their maximum age.

Row.title: Row
Row.description: The rows that make up a query result table.

ResultSetMetadata.title: ResultSetMetadata
ResultSetMetadata.description: The metadata that describes the column structure and data types of a table of query results. To return a ResultSetMetadata object, use GetQueryResults.

WorkGroupConfigurationUpdates.title: WorkGroupConfigurationUpdates
WorkGroupConfigurationUpdates.description: The configuration information that will be updated for this workgroup, which includes the location in Amazon S3 where query and calculation results are stored, the encryption option, if any, used for query results, whether the Amazon CloudWatch Metrics are enabled for the workgroup, whether the workgroup settings override the client-side settings, and the data usage limit for the amount of bytes scanned per query, if it is specified.

CalculationExecutionState1.title: CalculationExecutionState1
CalculationExecutionState1.description:
The state of the calculation execution. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The calculation is in the process of being created.

CREATED - The calculation has been created and is ready to run.

QUEUED - The calculation has been queued for processing.

RUNNING - The calculation is running.

CANCELING - A request to cancel the calculation has been received and the system is working to stop it.

CANCELED - The calculation is no longer running as the result of a cancel request.

COMPLETED - The calculation has completed without error.

FAILED - The calculation failed and is no longer running.

CalculationExecutionState3.title: CalculationExecutionState3
CalculationExecutionState3.description:
A filter for a specific calculation execution state. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The calculation is in the process of being created.

CREATED - The calculation has been created and is ready to run.

QUEUED - The calculation has been queued for processing.

RUNNING - The calculation is running.

CANCELING - A request to cancel the calculation has been received and the system is working to stop it.

CANCELED - The calculation is no longer running as the result of a cancel request.

COMPLETED - The calculation has completed without error.

FAILED - The calculation failed and is no longer running.

CalculationExecutionState4.title: CalculationExecutionState4
CalculationExecutionState4.description:
CREATING - The calculation is in the process of being created.

CREATED - The calculation has been created and is ready to run.

QUEUED - The calculation has been queued for processing.

RUNNING - The calculation is running.

CANCELING - A request to cancel the calculation has been received and the system is working to stop it.

CANCELED - The calculation is no longer running as the result of a cancel request.

COMPLETED - The calculation has completed without error.

FAILED - The calculation failed and is no longer running.

DataCatalogType1.title: DataCatalogType1
DataCatalogType1.description: The type of data catalog to create: LAMBDA for a federated catalog, HIVE for an external hive metastore, or GLUE for an Glue Data Catalog.

DataCatalogType3.title: DataCatalogType3
DataCatalogType3.description: Specifies the type of data catalog to update. Specify LAMBDA for a federated catalog, HIVE for an external hive metastore, or GLUE for an Glue Data Catalog.

EncryptionOption1.title: EncryptionOption1
EncryptionOption1.description:
Indicates whether Amazon S3 server-side encryption with Amazon S3-managed keys (SSE_S3), server-side encryption with KMS-managed keys (SSE_KMS), or client-side encryption with KMS-managed keys (CSE_KMS) is used.

If a query runs in a workgroup and the workgroup overrides client-side settings, then the workgroup's setting for encryption is used. It specifies whether query results must be encrypted, for all queries that run in this workgroup.

ExecutorState1.title: ExecutorState1
ExecutorState1.description:
A filter for a specific executor state. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The executor is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The executor has been started.

REGISTERED - The executor has been registered.

TERMINATING - The executor is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The executor is no longer running.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the executor is no longer running.

ExecutorState3.title: ExecutorState3
ExecutorState3.description:
The processing state of the executor. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The executor is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The executor has been started.

REGISTERED - The executor has been registered.

TERMINATING - The executor is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The executor is no longer running.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the executor is no longer running.

ExecutorType2.title: ExecutorType2
ExecutorType2.description: The type of executor used for the application (COORDINATOR, GATEWAY, or WORKER).

NotebookType1.title: NotebookType1
NotebookType1.description: The type of notebook. Currently, the only valid type is IPYNB.

NotebookType2.title: NotebookType2
NotebookType2.description: The notebook content type. Currently, the only valid type is IPYNB.

QueryExecutionState1.title: QueryExecutionState1
QueryExecutionState1.description:
The state of query execution. QUEUED indicates that the query has been submitted to the service, and Athena will execute the query as soon as resources are available. RUNNING indicates that the query is in execution phase. SUCCEEDED indicates that the query completed without errors. FAILED indicates that the query experienced an error and did not complete processing. CANCELLED indicates that a user input interrupted query execution.

Athena automatically retries your queries in cases of certain transient errors. As a result, you may see the query state transition from RUNNING or FAILED to QUEUED.

S3AclOption1.title: S3AclOption1
S3AclOption1.description: The Amazon S3 canned ACL that Athena should specify when storing query results. Currently the only supported canned ACL is BUCKET_OWNER_FULL_CONTROL. If a query runs in a workgroup and the workgroup overrides client-side settings, then the Amazon S3 canned ACL specified in the workgroup's settings is used for all queries that run in the workgroup. For more information about Amazon S3 canned ACLs, see Canned ACL in the Amazon S3 User Guide.

SessionState1.title: SessionState1
SessionState1.description:
The state of the session. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The session is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The session has been started.

IDLE - The session is able to accept a calculation.

BUSY - The session is processing another task and is unable to accept a calculation.

TERMINATING - The session is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The session and its resources are no longer running.

DEGRADED - The session has no healthy coordinators.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the session and its resources are no longer running.

SessionState3.title: SessionState3
SessionState3.description:
A filter for a specific session state. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The session is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The session has been started.

IDLE - The session is able to accept a calculation.

BUSY - The session is processing another task and is unable to accept a calculation.

TERMINATING - The session is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The session and its resources are no longer running.

DEGRADED - The session has no healthy coordinators.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the session and its resources are no longer running.

StatementType1.title: StatementType1
StatementType1.description: The type of query statement that was run. DDL indicates DDL query statements. DML indicates DML (Data Manipulation Language) query statements, such as CREATE TABLE AS SELECT. UTILITY indicates query statements other than DDL and DML, such as SHOW CREATE TABLE, or DESCRIBE TABLE.

WorkGroupState2.title: WorkGroupState2
WorkGroupState2.description: The workgroup state that will be updated for the given workgroup.

WorkGroupState3.title: WorkGroupState3
WorkGroupState3.description: The state of the workgroup: ENABLED or DISABLED.

Nodes.Text:
Amazon Athena is an interactive query service that lets you use standard SQL to analyze data directly in Amazon S3. You can point Athena at your data in Amazon S3 and run ad-hoc queries and get results in seconds. Athena is serverless, so there is no infrastructure to set up or manage. You pay only for the queries you run. Athena scales automatically—executing queries in parallel—so results are fast, even with large datasets and complex queries. For more information, see What is Amazon Athena in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

If you connect to Athena using the JDBC driver, use version 1.1.0 of the driver or later with the Amazon Athena API. Earlier version drivers do not support the API. For more information and to download the driver, see Accessing Amazon Athena with JDBC.

For code samples using the Amazon Web Services SDK for Java, see Examples and Code Samples in the Amazon Athena User Guide.
Amazon Web Services documentation: [https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/athena/)
Requirements

The SDK relies on **Node.js** and **npm** (to resolve dependencies). It also requires **Typescript version >=4.1**. You can download and install Node.js and [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/) from [the official Node.js website](https://nodejs.org/en/download/).
> **NOTE:** npm is installed by default when Node.js is installed.
Verify Successful Installation
Run the following commands in the command prompt or shell of your choice to check if Node.js and npm are successfully installed:
* Node.js: `node --version`
* npm: `npm --version`
Install Dependencies
- To resolve all dependencies, go to the **SDK root directory** and run the following command with npm:

```bash
npm install
```
- This will install all dependencies in the **node_modules** folder.
The following section explains how to use the generated library in a new project.
- Open an IDE/text editor for JavaScript like Visual Studio Code. The basic workflow presented here is also applicable if you prefer using a different editor or IDE.
- Click on **File** and select **Open Folder**. Select an empty folder of your project, the folder will become visible in the sidebar on the left.
- To initialize the Node project, click on **Terminal** and select **New Terminal**. Execute the following command in the terminal:

```bash
npm init --y
```

- The created project manages its dependencies using its `package.json` file. In order to add a dependency on the *Amazon AthenaLib* client library, double click on the ``` package.json ``` file in the bar on the left and add the dependency to the package in it.
- To install the package in the project, run the following command in the terminal:

```bash
npm install
```

Steps.Title: Add Dependencies to the Client Library

Nodes.Text: The following parameters are configurable for the API Client:
The API client can be initialized as follows:
Code-Based Client Initialization
Configuration-Based Client Initialization
See the [Configuration-Based Client Initialization]($h/__additional_documentation/Configuration/Configuration-Based%20Client%20Initialization) section for details.
Environment-Based Client Initialization
See the [Environment-Based Client Initialization]($h/__additional_documentation/Configuration/Environment-Based%20Client%20Initialization) section for details.

This API uses the following authentication schemes.
* [`hmac (Custom Header Signature)`]($h/__auth_CustomHeaderAuthentication)
Documentation for accessing and setting credentials for hmac.
**Note:** Auth credentials can be set using `customHeaderAuthenticationCredentials` object in the client.
You must provide credentials in the client as shown in the following code snippet.
* [API]($e/API)
The instance of the `ApiController` class can be created using the API Client.
const apiController = new ApiController(client);

Nodes.Title: Batch Get Named Query
Nodes.Name: batchGetNamedQuery
Nodes.Description: Returns the details of a single named query or a list of up to 50 queries, which you provide as an array of query ID strings. Requires you to have access to the workgroup in which the queries were saved. Use ListNamedQueriesInput to get the list of named query IDs in the specified workgroup. If information could not be retrieved for a submitted query ID, information about the query ID submitted is listed under UnprocessedNamedQueryId. Named queries differ from executed queries. Use BatchGetQueryExecutionInput to get details about each unique query execution, and ListQueryExecutionsInput to get a list of query execution IDs.

Nodes.Title: Batch Get Prepared Statement
Nodes.Name: batchGetPreparedStatement
Nodes.Description: Returns the details of a single prepared statement or a list of up to 256 prepared statements for the array of prepared statement names that you provide. Requires you to have access to the workgroup to which the prepared statements belong. If a prepared statement cannot be retrieved for the name specified, the statement is listed in UnprocessedPreparedStatementNames.

Nodes.Title: Get Query Results
Nodes.Name: getQueryResults
Nodes.Description:
Streams the results of a single query execution specified by QueryExecutionId from the Athena query results location in Amazon S3. For more information, see Working with query results, recent queries, and output files in the Amazon Athena User Guide. This request does not execute the query but returns results. Use StartQueryExecution to run a query.

To stream query results successfully, the IAM principal with permission to call GetQueryResults also must have permissions to the Amazon S3 GetObject action for the Athena query results location.

IAM principals with permission to the Amazon S3 GetObject action for the query results location are able to retrieve query results from Amazon S3 even if permission to the GetQueryResults action is denied. To restrict user or role access, ensure that Amazon S3 permissions to the Athena query location are denied.

Nodes.Title: List Query Executions
Nodes.Name: listQueryExecutions
Nodes.Description:
Provides a list of available query execution IDs for the queries in the specified workgroup. If a workgroup is not specified, returns a list of query execution IDs for the primary workgroup. Requires you to have access to the workgroup in which the queries ran.

For code samples using the Amazon Web Services SDK for Java, see Examples and Code Samples in the Amazon Athena User Guide.

Nodes.Title: Stop Calculation Execution
Nodes.Name: stopCalculationExecution
Nodes.Description:
Requests the cancellation of a calculation. A StopCalculationExecution call on a calculation that is already in a terminal state (for example, STOPPED, FAILED, or COMPLETED) succeeds but has no effect.

Cancelling a calculation is done on a best effort basis. If a calculation cannot be cancelled, you can be charged for its completion. If you are concerned about being charged for a calculation that cannot be cancelled, consider terminating the session in which the calculation is running.

Nodes.Title: Tag Resource
Nodes.Name: tagResource
Nodes.Description: Adds one or more tags to an Athena resource. A tag is a label that you assign to a resource. In Athena, a resource can be a workgroup or data catalog. Each tag consists of a key and an optional value, both of which you define. For example, you can use tags to categorize Athena workgroups or data catalogs by purpose, owner, or environment. Use a consistent set of tag keys to make it easier to search and filter workgroups or data catalogs in your account. For best practices, see Tagging Best Practices. Tag keys can be from 1 to 128 UTF-8 Unicode characters, and tag values can be from 0 to 256 UTF-8 Unicode characters. Tags can use letters and numbers representable in UTF-8, and the following characters: + - = . _ : / @. Tag keys and values are case-sensitive. Tag keys must be unique per resource. If you specify more than one tag, separate them by commas.

Nodes.Title: Terminate Session
Nodes.Name: terminateSession
Nodes.Description: Terminates an active session. A TerminateSession call on a session that is already inactive (for example, in a FAILED, TERMINATED or TERMINATING state) succeeds but has no effect. Calculations running in the session when TerminateSession is called are forcefully stopped, but may display as FAILED instead of STOPPED.

Nodes.Title: Batch Get Named Query Input
Nodes.Name: BatchGetNamedQueryInput
Nodes.Description: Contains an array of named query IDs.

Nodes.Title: Batch Get Query Execution Input
Nodes.Name: BatchGetQueryExecutionInput
Nodes.Description: Contains an array of query execution IDs.

Nodes.Title: Acl Configuration
Nodes.Name: AclConfiguration
Nodes.Description: Indicates that an Amazon S3 canned ACL should be set to control ownership of stored query results. When Athena stores query results in Amazon S3, the canned ACL is set with the x-amz-acl request header. For more information about S3 Object Ownership, see Object Ownership settings in the Amazon S3 User Guide.

Nodes.Title: Application DPU Sizes
Nodes.Name: ApplicationDPUSizes
Nodes.Description: Contains the application runtime IDs and their supported DPU sizes.

Nodes.Title: Athena Error
Nodes.Name: AthenaError
Nodes.Description: Provides information about an Athena query error. The AthenaError feature provides standardized error information to help you understand failed queries and take steps after a query failure occurs. AthenaError includes an ErrorCategory field that specifies whether the cause of the failed query is due to system error, user error, or other error.

Nodes.Title: Named Query
Nodes.Name: NamedQuery
Nodes.Description: A query, where QueryString contains the SQL statements that make up the query.

Nodes.Title: Unprocessed Named Query Id
Nodes.Name: UnprocessedNamedQueryId
Nodes.Description: Information about a named query ID that could not be processed.

Nodes.Title: Prepared Statement
Nodes.Name: PreparedStatement
Nodes.Description: A prepared SQL statement for use with Athena.

Nodes.Title: Unprocessed Prepared Statement Name
Nodes.Name: UnprocessedPreparedStatementName
Nodes.Description: The name of a prepared statement that could not be returned.

Nodes.Title: Query Execution
Nodes.Name: QueryExecution
Nodes.Description: Information about a single instance of a query execution.

Nodes.Title: Unprocessed Query Execution Id
Nodes.Name: UnprocessedQueryExecutionId
Nodes.Description: Describes a query execution that failed to process.

Nodes.Title: Calculation Configuration
Nodes.Name: CalculationConfiguration
Nodes.Description: Contains configuration information for the calculation.

Nodes.Title: Calculation Result
Nodes.Name: CalculationResult
Nodes.Description: Contains information about an application-specific calculation result.

Nodes.Title: Calculation Statistics
Nodes.Name: CalculationStatistics
Nodes.Description: Contains statistics for a notebook calculation.

Nodes.Title: Calculation Status
Nodes.Name: CalculationStatus
Nodes.Description: Contains information about the status of a notebook calculation.

Nodes.Title: Calculation Summary
Nodes.Name: CalculationSummary
Nodes.Description: Summary information for a notebook calculation.

Nodes.Title: Column
Nodes.Name: Column
Nodes.Description: Contains metadata for a column in a table.

Nodes.Title: Column Info
Nodes.Name: ColumnInfo
Nodes.Description: Information about the columns in a query execution result.

Nodes.Title: Tag
Nodes.Name: Tag
Nodes.Description: A label that you assign to a resource. In Athena, a resource can be a workgroup or data catalog. Each tag consists of a key and an optional value, both of which you define. For example, you can use tags to categorize Athena workgroups or data catalogs by purpose, owner, or environment. Use a consistent set of tag keys to make it easier to search and filter workgroups or data catalogs in your account. For best practices, see Tagging Best Practices. Tag keys can be from 1 to 128 UTF-8 Unicode characters, and tag values can be from 0 to 256 UTF-8 Unicode characters. Tags can use letters and numbers representable in UTF-8, and the following characters: + - = . _ : / @. Tag keys and values are case-sensitive. Tag keys must be unique per resource. If you specify more than one tag, separate them by commas.

Nodes.Title: Work Group Configuration
Nodes.Name: WorkGroupConfiguration
Nodes.Description: The configuration of the workgroup, which includes the location in Amazon S3 where query and calculation results are stored, the encryption option, if any, used for query and calculation results, whether the Amazon CloudWatch Metrics are enabled for the workgroup and whether workgroup settings override query settings, and the data usage limits for the amount of data scanned per query or per workgroup. The workgroup settings override is specified in EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration (true/false) in the WorkGroupConfiguration. See WorkGroupConfiguration$EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration.

Nodes.Title: Customer Content Encryption Configuration
Nodes.Name: CustomerContentEncryptionConfiguration
Nodes.Description: Specifies the KMS key that is used to encrypt the user's data stores in Athena.

Nodes.Title: Data Catalog
Nodes.Name: DataCatalog
Nodes.Description:
Contains information about a data catalog in an Amazon Web Services account.

In the Athena console, data catalogs are listed as "data sources" on the Data sources page under the Data source name column.

Nodes.Title: Data Catalog Summary
Nodes.Name: DataCatalogSummary
Nodes.Description: The summary information for the data catalog, which includes its name and type.

Nodes.Title: Database
Nodes.Name: Database
Nodes.Description: Contains metadata information for a database in a data catalog.

Nodes.Title: Datum
Nodes.Name: Datum
Nodes.Description: A piece of data (a field in the table).

Nodes.Title: Encryption Configuration
Nodes.Name: EncryptionConfiguration
Nodes.Description: If query and calculation results are encrypted in Amazon S3, indicates the encryption option used (for example, SSE_KMS or CSE_KMS) and key information.

Nodes.Title: Engine Configuration
Nodes.Name: EngineConfiguration
Nodes.Description: Contains data processing unit (DPU) configuration settings and parameter mappings for a notebook engine.

Nodes.Title: Engine Version
Nodes.Name: EngineVersion
Nodes.Description: The Athena engine version for running queries, or the PySpark engine version for running sessions.

Nodes.Title: Executors Summary
Nodes.Name: ExecutorsSummary
Nodes.Description: Contains summary information about an executor.

Nodes.Title: Notebook Metadata
Nodes.Name: NotebookMetadata
Nodes.Description: Contains metadata for notebook, including the notebook name, ID, workgroup, and time created.

Nodes.Title: Filter Definition
Nodes.Name: FilterDefinition
Nodes.Description: A string for searching notebook names.

Nodes.Title: Result Set
Nodes.Name: ResultSet
Nodes.Description: The metadata and rows that make up a query result set. The metadata describes the column structure and data types. To return a ResultSet object, use GetQueryResults.

Fields.Name: timeline
Fields.Description: Timeline statistics such as query queue time, planning time, execution time, service processing time, and total execution time.

Fields.Name: rows
Fields.Description: Statistics such as input rows and bytes read by the query, rows and bytes output by the query, and the number of rows written by the query.

Nodes.Title: Query Runtime Statistics
Nodes.Name: QueryRuntimeStatistics
Nodes.Description: The query execution timeline, statistics on input and output rows and bytes, and the different query stages that form the query execution plan.

Fields.Name: encryptionConfiguration
Fields.Description: If query and calculation results are encrypted in Amazon S3, indicates the encryption option used (for example, SSE_KMS or CSE_KMS) and key information.

Nodes.Title: Session Configuration
Nodes.Name: SessionConfiguration
Nodes.Description: Contains session configuration information.

Nodes.Title: Session Status
Nodes.Name: SessionStatus
Nodes.Description: Contains information about the status of a session.

Nodes.Title: Session Statistics
Nodes.Name: SessionStatistics
Nodes.Description: Contains statistics for a session.

Nodes.Title: Table Metadata
Nodes.Name: TableMetadata
Nodes.Description: Contains metadata for a table.

Nodes.Title: Work Group
Nodes.Name: WorkGroup
Nodes.Description: A workgroup, which contains a name, description, creation time, state, and other configuration, listed under WorkGroup$Configuration. Each workgroup enables you to isolate queries for you or your group of users from other queries in the same account, to configure the query results location and the encryption configuration (known as workgroup settings), to enable sending query metrics to Amazon CloudWatch, and to establish per-query data usage control limits for all queries in a workgroup. The workgroup settings override is specified in EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration (true/false) in the WorkGroupConfiguration. See WorkGroupConfiguration$EnforceWorkGroupConfiguration.

Nodes.Title: Notebook Session Summary
Nodes.Name: NotebookSessionSummary
Nodes.Description: Contains the notebook session ID and notebook session creation time.

Nodes.Title: Prepared Statement Summary
Nodes.Name: PreparedStatementSummary
Nodes.Description: The name and last modified time of the prepared statement.

Nodes.Title: Session Summary
Nodes.Name: SessionSummary
Nodes.Description: Contains summary information about a session.

Nodes.Title: Work Group Summary
Nodes.Name: WorkGroupSummary
Nodes.Description: The summary information for the workgroup, which includes its name, state, description, and the date and time it was created.

Nodes.Title: Result Configuration
Nodes.Name: ResultConfiguration
Nodes.Description: The location in Amazon S3 where query and calculation results are stored and the encryption option, if any, used for query and calculation results. These are known as "client-side settings". If workgroup settings override client-side settings, then the query uses the workgroup settings.

Nodes.Title: Result Reuse Configuration
Nodes.Name: ResultReuseConfiguration
Nodes.Description: Specifies the query result reuse behavior for the query.

Nodes.Title: Query Execution Context
Nodes.Name: QueryExecutionContext
Nodes.Description: The database and data catalog context in which the query execution occurs.

Nodes.Title: Query Execution Status
Nodes.Name: QueryExecutionStatus
Nodes.Description: The completion date, current state, submission time, and state change reason (if applicable) for the query execution.

Nodes.Title: Query Execution Statistics
Nodes.Name: QueryExecutionStatistics
Nodes.Description: The amount of data scanned during the query execution and the amount of time that it took to execute, and the type of statement that was run.

Nodes.Title: Result Reuse Information
Nodes.Name: ResultReuseInformation
Nodes.Description: Contains information about whether the result of a previous query was reused.

Nodes.Title: Query Runtime Statistics Timeline
Nodes.Name: QueryRuntimeStatisticsTimeline
Nodes.Description: Timeline statistics such as query queue time, planning time, execution time, service processing time, and total execution time.

Nodes.Title: Query Runtime Statistics Rows
Nodes.Name: QueryRuntimeStatisticsRows
Nodes.Description: Statistics such as input rows and bytes read by the query, rows and bytes output by the query, and the number of rows written by the query.

Nodes.Title: Query Stage
Nodes.Name: QueryStage
Nodes.Description: Stage statistics such as input and output rows and bytes, execution time and stage state. This information also includes substages and the query stage plan.

Nodes.Title: Query Stage Plan Node
Nodes.Name: QueryStagePlanNode
Nodes.Description: Stage plan information such as name, identifier, sub plans, and remote sources.

Nodes.Title: Result Configuration Updates
Nodes.Name: ResultConfigurationUpdates
Nodes.Description: The information about the updates in the query results, such as output location and encryption configuration for the query results.

Nodes.Title: Result Reuse by Age Configuration
Nodes.Name: ResultReuseByAgeConfiguration
Nodes.Description: Specifies whether previous query results are reused, and if so, their maximum age.

Nodes.Title: Row
Nodes.Name: Row
Nodes.Description: The rows that make up a query result table.

Nodes.Title: Result Set Metadata
Nodes.Name: ResultSetMetadata
Nodes.Description: The metadata that describes the column structure and data types of a table of query results. To return a ResultSetMetadata object, use GetQueryResults.

Fields.Name: customerContentEncryptionConfiguration
Fields.Description: Specifies the KMS key that is used to encrypt the user's data stores in Athena.

Nodes.Title: Work Group Configuration Updates
Nodes.Name: WorkGroupConfigurationUpdates
Nodes.Description: The configuration information that will be updated for this workgroup, which includes the location in Amazon S3 where query and calculation results are stored, the encryption option, if any, used for query results, whether the Amazon CloudWatch Metrics are enabled for the workgroup, whether the workgroup settings override the client-side settings, and the data usage limit for the amount of bytes scanned per query, if it is specified.

Fields.Name: customerContentEncryptionConfiguration
Fields.Description: Specifies the KMS key that is used to encrypt the user's data stores in Athena.

Fields.Name: timeline
Fields.Description: Timeline statistics such as query queue time, planning time, execution time, service processing time, and total execution time.

Fields.Name: rows
Fields.Description: Statistics such as input rows and bytes read by the query, rows and bytes output by the query, and the number of rows written by the query.

Fields.Name: encryptionConfiguration
Fields.Description: If query and calculation results are encrypted in Amazon S3, indicates the encryption option used (for example, SSE_KMS or CSE_KMS) and key information.

import { S3AclOptionEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const s3AclOption = S3AclOptionEnum.BUCKETOWNERFULLCONTROL;

import { CalculationExecutionStateEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const calculationExecutionState = CalculationExecutionStateEnum.COMPLETED;

import { ColumnNullableEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const columnNullable = ColumnNullableEnum.NOTNULL;

import { DataCatalogTypeEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const dataCatalogType = DataCatalogTypeEnum.HIVE;

import { EncryptionOptionEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const encryptionOption = EncryptionOptionEnum.SSES3;

import { ExecutorStateEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const executorState = ExecutorStateEnum.TERMINATED;

import { ExecutorTypeEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const executorType = ExecutorTypeEnum.GATEWAY;

import { NotebookTypeEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const notebookType = NotebookTypeEnum.IPYNB;

import { SessionStateEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const sessionState = SessionStateEnum.TERMINATING;

import { StatementTypeEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const statementType = StatementTypeEnum.DDL;

import { QueryExecutionStateEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const queryExecutionState = QueryExecutionStateEnum.CANCELLED;

import { WorkGroupStateEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const workGroupState = WorkGroupStateEnum.ENABLED;

Nodes.Title: Calculation Execution State 1
Nodes.Name: CalculationExecutionState1Enum
Nodes.Description:
The state of the calculation execution. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The calculation is in the process of being created.

CREATED - The calculation has been created and is ready to run.

QUEUED - The calculation has been queued for processing.

RUNNING - The calculation is running.

CANCELING - A request to cancel the calculation has been received and the system is working to stop it.

CANCELED - The calculation is no longer running as the result of a cancel request.

COMPLETED - The calculation has completed without error.

FAILED - The calculation failed and is no longer running.

Example.Text:
import { CalculationExecutionState1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const calculationExecutionState1 = CalculationExecutionState1Enum.COMPLETED;

Nodes.Title: Calculation Execution State 3
Nodes.Name: CalculationExecutionState3Enum
Nodes.Description:
A filter for a specific calculation execution state. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The calculation is in the process of being created.

CREATED - The calculation has been created and is ready to run.

QUEUED - The calculation has been queued for processing.

RUNNING - The calculation is running.

CANCELING - A request to cancel the calculation has been received and the system is working to stop it.

CANCELED - The calculation is no longer running as the result of a cancel request.

COMPLETED - The calculation has completed without error.

FAILED - The calculation failed and is no longer running.

Example.Text:
import { CalculationExecutionState3Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const calculationExecutionState3 = CalculationExecutionState3Enum.COMPLETED;

Nodes.Title: Calculation Execution State 4
Nodes.Name: CalculationExecutionState4Enum
Nodes.Description:
CREATING - The calculation is in the process of being created.

CREATED - The calculation has been created and is ready to run.

QUEUED - The calculation has been queued for processing.

RUNNING - The calculation is running.

CANCELING - A request to cancel the calculation has been received and the system is working to stop it.

CANCELED - The calculation is no longer running as the result of a cancel request.

COMPLETED - The calculation has completed without error.

FAILED - The calculation failed and is no longer running.

Example.Text:
import { CalculationExecutionState4Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const calculationExecutionState4 = CalculationExecutionState4Enum.CANCELING;

Nodes.Title: Column Nullable 2
Nodes.Name: ColumnNullable2Enum
Nodes.Description: Indicates the column's nullable status.

Example.Text:
import { ColumnNullable2Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const columnNullable2 = ColumnNullable2Enum.NOTNULL;

Nodes.Title: Data Catalog Type 1
Nodes.Name: DataCatalogType1Enum
Nodes.Description: The type of data catalog to create: LAMBDA for a federated catalog, HIVE for an external hive metastore, or GLUE for an Glue Data Catalog.

Example.Text:
import { DataCatalogType1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const dataCatalogType1 = DataCatalogType1Enum.GLUE;

Nodes.Title: Data Catalog Type 2
Nodes.Name: DataCatalogType2Enum
Nodes.Description: The data catalog type.

Example.Text:
import { DataCatalogType2Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const dataCatalogType2 = DataCatalogType2Enum.LAMBDA;

Nodes.Title: Data Catalog Type 3
Nodes.Name: DataCatalogType3Enum
Nodes.Description: Specifies the type of data catalog to update. Specify LAMBDA for a federated catalog, HIVE for an external hive metastore, or GLUE for an Glue Data Catalog.

Example.Text:
import { DataCatalogType3Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const dataCatalogType3 = DataCatalogType3Enum.LAMBDA;

Nodes.Title: Encryption Option 1
Nodes.Name: EncryptionOption1Enum
Nodes.Description:
Indicates whether Amazon S3 server-side encryption with Amazon S3-managed keys (SSE_S3), server-side encryption with KMS-managed keys (SSE_KMS), or client-side encryption with KMS-managed keys (CSE_KMS) is used.

If a query runs in a workgroup and the workgroup overrides client-side settings, then the workgroup's setting for encryption is used. It specifies whether query results must be encrypted, for all queries that run in this workgroup.

Example.Text:
import { EncryptionOption1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const encryptionOption1 = EncryptionOption1Enum.CSEKMS;

Nodes.Title: Executor State 1
Nodes.Name: ExecutorState1Enum
Nodes.Description:
A filter for a specific executor state. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The executor is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The executor has been started.

REGISTERED - The executor has been registered.

TERMINATING - The executor is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The executor is no longer running.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the executor is no longer running.

Example.Text:
import { ExecutorState1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const executorState1 = ExecutorState1Enum.CREATING;

Nodes.Title: Executor State 3
Nodes.Name: ExecutorState3Enum
Nodes.Description:
The processing state of the executor. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The executor is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The executor has been started.

REGISTERED - The executor has been registered.

TERMINATING - The executor is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The executor is no longer running.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the executor is no longer running.

Example.Text:
import { ExecutorState3Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const executorState3 = ExecutorState3Enum.CREATING;

Nodes.Title: Executor Type 2
Nodes.Name: ExecutorType2Enum
Nodes.Description: The type of executor used for the application (COORDINATOR, GATEWAY, or WORKER).

Example.Text:
import { ExecutorType2Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const executorType2 = ExecutorType2Enum.COORDINATOR;

Nodes.Title: Notebook Type 1
Nodes.Name: NotebookType1Enum
Nodes.Description: The type of notebook. Currently, the only valid type is IPYNB.

Example.Text:
import { NotebookType1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const notebookType1 = NotebookType1Enum.IPYNB;

Nodes.Title: Notebook Type 2
Nodes.Name: NotebookType2Enum
Nodes.Description: The notebook content type. Currently, the only valid type is IPYNB.

Example.Text:
import { NotebookType2Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const notebookType2 = NotebookType2Enum.IPYNB;

Nodes.Title: Query Execution State 1
Nodes.Name: QueryExecutionState1Enum
Nodes.Description:
The state of query execution. QUEUED indicates that the query has been submitted to the service, and Athena will execute the query as soon as resources are available. RUNNING indicates that the query is in execution phase. SUCCEEDED indicates that the query completed without errors. FAILED indicates that the query experienced an error and did not complete processing. CANCELLED indicates that a user input interrupted query execution.

Athena automatically retries your queries in cases of certain transient errors. As a result, you may see the query state transition from RUNNING or FAILED to QUEUED.

Example.Text:
import { QueryExecutionState1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const queryExecutionState1 = QueryExecutionState1Enum.CANCELLED;

import { RegionEnum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

Nodes.Title: S3 Acl Option 1
Nodes.Name: S3AclOption1Enum
Nodes.Description: The Amazon S3 canned ACL that Athena should specify when storing query results. Currently the only supported canned ACL is BUCKET_OWNER_FULL_CONTROL. If a query runs in a workgroup and the workgroup overrides client-side settings, then the Amazon S3 canned ACL specified in the workgroup's settings is used for all queries that run in the workgroup. For more information about Amazon S3 canned ACLs, see Canned ACL in the Amazon S3 User Guide.

Example.Text:
import { S3AclOption1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const s3AclOption1 = S3AclOption1Enum.BUCKETOWNERFULLCONTROL;

Nodes.Title: Session State 1
Nodes.Name: SessionState1Enum
Nodes.Description:
The state of the session. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The session is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The session has been started.

IDLE - The session is able to accept a calculation.

BUSY - The session is processing another task and is unable to accept a calculation.

TERMINATING - The session is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The session and its resources are no longer running.

DEGRADED - The session has no healthy coordinators.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the session and its resources are no longer running.

Example.Text:
import { SessionState1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const sessionState1 = SessionState1Enum.DEGRADED;

Nodes.Title: Session State 3
Nodes.Name: SessionState3Enum
Nodes.Description:
A filter for a specific session state. A description of each state follows.

CREATING - The session is being started, including acquiring resources.

CREATED - The session has been started.

IDLE - The session is able to accept a calculation.

BUSY - The session is processing another task and is unable to accept a calculation.

TERMINATING - The session is in the process of shutting down.

TERMINATED - The session and its resources are no longer running.

DEGRADED - The session has no healthy coordinators.

FAILED - Due to a failure, the session and its resources are no longer running.

Example.Text:
import { SessionState3Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const sessionState3 = SessionState3Enum.DEGRADED;

Nodes.Title: Statement Type 1
Nodes.Name: StatementType1Enum
Nodes.Description: The type of query statement that was run. DDL indicates DDL query statements. DML indicates DML (Data Manipulation Language) query statements, such as CREATE TABLE AS SELECT. UTILITY indicates query statements other than DDL and DML, such as SHOW CREATE TABLE, or DESCRIBE TABLE.

Example.Text:
import { StatementType1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const statementType1 = StatementType1Enum.DDL;

Nodes.Title: Work Group State 1
Nodes.Name: WorkGroupState1Enum
Nodes.Description: The state of the workgroup.

Example.Text:
import { WorkGroupState1Enum } from 'amazon-athenalib';

const workGroupState1 = WorkGroupState1Enum.ENABLED;

Nodes.Name: WorkGroupState2Enum
Nodes.Description: The workgroup state that will be updated for the given workgroup.

Example.Text:

const workGroupState2 = WorkGroupState2Enum.ENABLED;

Nodes.Name: WorkGroupState3Enum
Nodes.Description: The state of the workgroup: ENABLED or DISABLED.

Nodes.Text: Represents the HTTP client configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data: Custom http agent to be used when performing http requests.
Custom https agent to be used when performing http requests.
Proxy server configurations to route both HTTP and HTTPS requests through a proxy.

Nodes.Text: Represents the retry configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data:
Whether to retry on request timeout.
*Default*: `true`

Interval before next retry. Used in calculation of wait time for next request in case of failure.
*Default*: `1`

Overall wait time for the requests getting retried.
*Default*: `0`

Used in calculation of wait time for next request in case of failure.
*Default*: `2`

Nodes.Text: Represents the proxy server configurations for API calls.
Properties

Data: The port to connect to the proxy server.

Nodes.Text: ProxyAuth
Client Initialization with Proxy Configuration
To configure the SDK to use a proxy server, initialize the proxy configuration during client setup as shown in the Usage Example.

CodeExamples.Title: Usage Example

Nodes.Text: The SDK client can be initialized directly from a JSON configuration file using the `fromJsonConfig` static method available on the `Client` class.` This allows you to store and load configuration settings from JSON files, making it easier to organize and manage different configurations across environments.
Node.js Environment
In Node.js environments, you can use the `fs` module to read the configuration file from the file system.
Browser Environment
In browser environments, you can use the `import` function to load the configuration file.
Example Configuration File

The SDK client can be initialized directly from environment variables using the `fromEnvironment` static method available on the `Client` class. This allows you to configure the SDK through environment variables, making it easier to manage different settings across different environments.
Node.js Environment
In Node.js environments, you can use the `dotenv` package to load environment variables from a `.env` file and pass `process.env` to the configuration.
The `fromEnvironment` method accepts an optional parameter for environment variables. If not provided, it defaults to `process.env`.
Browser Environment

In browser environments, you can pass configuration values directly as key-value pairs to the `fromEnvironment` method.  Since `process.env` is not available in browsers, environment variables must be passed as an object.
Example .env File

# Basic Configuration
TIMEOUT=30000
ENVIRONMENT=production
REGION=us-east-1

# Authentication Credentials
AUTHORIZATION=authorization

# Retry Configuration
MAX_NUMBER_OF_RETRIES=3
RETRY_ON_TIMEOUT=true
RETRY_INTERVAL=10
MAX_RETRY_WAIT_TIME=120
RETRY_BACKOFF_FACTOR=2
HTTP_STATUS_CODES_TO_RETRY=408,413,429,500,502
HTTP_METHODS_TO_RETRY=GET,PUT,DELETE

# Proxy Settings
PROXY_ADDRESS=http://localhost:3000
PROXY_PORT=8080
PROXY_AUTH_USERNAME=username
PROXY_AUTH_PASSWORD=password
Represents an HTTP request.
Properties

Data: ```GET | DELETE | HEAD | OPTIONS | POST | PUT | PATCH | LINK | UNLINK```
Request URL including the query part.

Nodes.Text: HttpRequestBody
Represents the HTTP request body, which can be of different types.
HttpRequestTextBody
HttpRequestUrlEncodedFormBody
HttpRequestMultipartFormBody
HttpRequestStreamBody
Represents the result of an API call, including response metadata and the returned data of type `T`.
Properties

Data: Original request that resulted in this response.

Nodes.Text:
Thrown when the HTTP status code is not okay.

The ApiError extends the ApiResponse interface, so all ApiResponse properties are available.
Properties

Data: Original request that resulted in this response.

Nodes.Text: This developer portal is powered by **APIMatic**. The same platform that generated this experience can turn any OpenAPI specification into a complete developer portal — interactive documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, live API testing, and AI-assisted onboarding — in minutes.

Context Plugins load your full API context — docs, SDKs, code samples, and setup instructions — into AI coding environments like Claude Code and Cursor, so developers integrate your API through natural language instead of a wall of reference docs. A built-in portal copilot is available too.

API Reference documentation with language‑specific docs and SDK guides — including idiomatic, ready-to-run code samples complete with imports across all seven SDK languages. Developers can explore your API structure before writing any code.
Built-in testing interface supporting all major authentication methods. Developers can make real API calls, see actual responses, and validate their integration approach.
Production-ready client libraries in Java, Python, TypeScript, C#, PHP, Ruby, and Go. Each SDK includes comprehensive documentation, code samples, and follows language-specific best practices.
Step-by-step onboarding flows to help developers implement key API usecases faster.
Full control over colors, fonts, logos, and layout. Add custom documentation pages, modify navigation, and ensure the portal reflects your brand identity.
Bring your API to APIMatic and ship a developer portal like this one in minutes.
See how APIMatic turns your API specification into a branded, AI-ready developer portal.
Dive into the Advanced Features Guide to set up and customize your own portal.

Nodes.Title: How quickly can we launch our Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: You can generate and publish your Developer Portal in minutes. Simply upload your OpenAPI specification, customize the look and feel to match your brand, and publish instantly - either as a standalone site or embedded into your existing Developer Portal.

Nodes.Title: How does the Portal stay current as our API evolves?

Nodes.Text: APIMatic automatically updates your portal when your API specification changes. You can re-import updated API definitions, and the portal will regenerate with the latest endpoints, parameters, and documentation while preserving your customizations.

Nodes.Title: What level of customization and control do we have?

Nodes.Text: You can completely customize the look, feel and behavior of your Developer Experience Portal, including primary colors, themes, custom sections, navigation links, custom URL slugs, and branding. You can also choose to host it on your own custom domain or embed it seamlessly into your existing documentation site using a simple HTML script.

Nodes.Title: Is there a way we can integrate this with our existing Developer Portal?

Nodes.Text: Absolutely. APIMatic provides embeddable portal components that can be seamlessly integrated into your existing documentation sites using a simple HTML script. You can also choose to host the portal separately on your own domain while maintaining your existing portal structure.

Nodes.Title: What AI capabilities does this Developer Portal have?

Nodes.Text: **Context Plugins** let developers connect your API to the AI coding assistant they already use (Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Claude, and more) to search, ask questions, and generate working integration code grounded in your live specification.

The portal also includes a built-in **API Copilot** that answers questions from your API reference and product documentation, and an **AI Assist Menu** that sends any documentation page straight to your preferred AI tool as context with one click.
Automatic **LLMs.txt generation** and per-page Markdown exports make your documentation AI-accessible to tools like Cursor, GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and Claude.
